CONTENT: IIT 4.0 Integration — Phase 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-3)
Implement PhiCalculator class (iit/phi_calculator.py)
Implement simple_phi() — cosine similarity in projected space
Implement _ksg_phi() — KSG mutual information estimator
Implement calculate_batch_phi() for batch processing

Train CCA projection matrices on existing dataset
Export text + image embeddings for all products with both
Run sklearn CCA to learn (384→256) and (512→256) projections
Save projection matrices to models/phi_projections.pkl
Validate projections: check correlation on held-out set
Modify storage/vector_search.py — add search_with_phi()
Add PhiCalculator initialization to VectorSearch.__init__()
Implement search_with_phi() returning both embeddings per row
Calculate Φ for each result and compute final_score = α·cosine + β·Φ
Re-rank results by final_score and return top-k
Create new API endpoint POST /api/search/phi
Add PhiSearchRequest schema (query + phi_weight + limit)
Add phi and cosine_score fields to SearchResult schema
Wire router to call search_with_phi()
Build frontend PhiBadge component (vv-web)
Create PhiBadge.tsx with color-coded Φ percentage

Phase 1 Validation
Calculate Φ scores for all products with both embeddings
Analyze Φ distribution — verify normal distribution centered ~0.5
Manual quality check: do high-Φ results feel more coherent?
Statistical validation: Φ correlation with category precision
Create phi_scores database table with indexes
IIT 4.0 Integration — Phase 2: Search Quality (Weeks 3-5)
Implement Φ-weighted ranking in production API
Add phi_weight parameter to search endpoint (default 0.3)
Cache Φ scores in phi_scores table for fast retrieval
Update API to return explainability object in responses
Build A/B testing infrastructure
Implement ABTest class (control vs treatment)
Run A/B test: phi_weight=0 (control) vs phi_weight=0.3 (treatment)
Test with 14 standard queries × 10 trials each
Analyze results: compute improvement and p-value
Search quality regression testing
Run existing search quality test suite with Φ-weighted ranking
Verify vibe query scores improve +5-10%
Verify no regression on era/culture queries
Document score changes across all 14 query categories
User explainability UI
Add integration_level label (high/medium/low) to search results
Add explanation text describing why image and text agree/disagree
Verify >70% of users understand Φ explanation (informal study)
IIT 4.0 Integration — Phase 3: Attribute Optimization (Weeks 5-7)
Field Attribution Analysis
Implement measure_field_contribution() — ablation per enrichment field
Build rich text with all 23 fields as baseline
For each field: rebuild rich text without it and measure Φ drop
Run analysis across 100-1000 products
Produce field importance ranking (sorted by average Φ contribution)
Maximal Complex Identification
Implement find_maximal_complex() — greedy search for minimal high-Φ field set
For each product: find subset achieving >95% of max Φ
Store maximal complexes in maximal_complexes database table
Analyze: how many fields needed on average? Which fields always appear?
Optimized Rich Text Construction
Implement build_rich_text_optimized() using only maximal complex fields
Add fallback to top-8 global fields when per-product complex unavailable
Re-embed 1000 products using optimized rich text
Compare Φ distribution: optimized vs baseline (expect +0.05-0.10)
Run search quality suite and verify vibe query improvement +3-5%
Update enrichment prompt to prioritize high-Φ fields
Modify Claude enrichment prompt with PRIORITY vs SECONDARY field sections
Re-run enrichment on sample products to verify prompt change
IIT 4.0 Integration — Phase 4: Complex Discovery (Weeks 7-10)
Combinatorial Φ Analysis
Implement discover_complexes() — test attribute combos for high Φ
Define semantic_fields list (era/decade/vibe/silhouette/colors/material)
Test all combinations of size 2-5 with min_support=10
Filter: remove subsets when superset has similar Φ
Produce ranked list of discovered complexes with Φ and support counts
Complex Ontology Construction
Implement ComplexOntology class with inverted index
Implement search_complexes() to find matching complexes by attributes
Create discovered_complexes database table with JSONB attributes
Name discovered complexes (e.g. GothVictorian / RomanticCottagecore)
Complex-Based Search
Implement search_by_complex() — search products by complex name
Build query text from complex attributes for pgvector search
Re-rank by complex alignment score (0.5*cosine + 0.5*alignment)
Create API endpoints: GET /api/complexes and POST /api/search/complex
Complex Discovery UI
Build "Discover Aesthetics" page showing all complexes
Show complex name / Φ score / support count / attribute summary
Add "View Items" link to browse products in each complex
Phase 4 Validation
Manual review: top 20 complexes represent coherent aesthetic concepts?
Verify intra-complex Φ > inter-complex Φ (clusters are distinct)
Verify complex search precision > generic search precision
User study: >80% agree complex products belong together
IIT 4.0 Integration — Phase 5: Adaptive Weighting (Weeks 10-12)
Query Φ Analysis
Implement analyze_query_phi() for text+image and text-only queries
Implement infer_query_modality_balance() heuristic for text-only queries
Classify queries as high-Φ (visual+semantic) vs low-Φ (abstract)
Adaptive Weighting Function
Implement adaptive_search() with dynamic text/image weight selection
High Φ query → balanced (0.5/0.5), low Φ → favor dominant modality
Implement merge_results() to combine text and image search results
Wire adaptive search into API as optional mode
Phase 5 A/B Test
A/B test: fixed weighting (control) vs adaptive weighting (treatment)
Test high-Φ queries ("red silk evening dress")
Test low-Φ queries ("dark academia aesthetic")
Verify low-Φ queries improve +5% without hurting high-Φ queries
IIT 4.0 Integration — CNN Integration
Phase 1: Multi-Task Attribute CNN
Define FashionAttributeCNN model (ResNet50 backbone + task heads)
Implement silhouette head (12 classes), neckline (8), pattern (6), length (5)
Implement color head (regression to RGB palette — 5 colors × 3 channels)
Implement multitask_loss with per-task weighting
Prepare training data from Claude enrichment labels
Create train/val/test splits (70/15/15)
Train CNN and evaluate accuracy per task head
Validate: silhouette >70% / neckline >60% / pattern >75% accuracy
Create cnn_predictions database table for storing CNN results
Phase 2: CLIP Fine-Tuning
Implement VintageCLIP class with contrastive_loss (InfoNCE)
Fine-tune CLIP on (image, rich_text) pairs from dataset
Training setup: 10-20 epochs, lr=1e-5, batch=32
Compare pretrained vs fine-tuned CLIP on Precision@5 and Recall@10
Measure Φ improvement: expect average Φ from ~0.52 to >0.60
Phase 3: Era Classification CNN
Implement EraClassifier model (EfficientNet-B3 backbone + 8-era classifier)
Train on Met Museum items with verified dates
Augmentation: horizontal flip / rotation / color jitter
Test on Smithsonian items (cross-museum generalization)
Report top-1 and top-3 accuracy (goal: top-1 >80% on Met)
Phase 4: Visual-Text Attribute Fusion
Implement fuse_attributes() — Φ-based vision-text fusion
Handle 3 cases: consensus, vision_primary, text_fallback
Implement calculate_attribute_phi() using semantic similarity
Create fused_attributes database table
Integrate fused attributes into search ranking
CNN-IIT Integration
Approach 1: Add phi_attributes to ranking score (α·cosine + β·Φ_emb + γ·Φ_attr)
Approach 2: Use Φ analysis to prune low-Φ CNN task heads
Approach 3: Include CNN attributes in complex discovery
Approach 4: Use CNN confidence to dynamically weight vision vs text

Create cnn_predictions table (per-attribute predictions + confidence)
Create fused_attributes table (fused values + phi + source per attribute)
Add indexes on phi_score columns
Configuration
Create config/iit.yaml with phi_calculator / search / complex settings
Add phi_weight default to search config (0.3)
Add complex_discovery thresholds (min_support=10 / min_phi=0.65)
Install new dependencies
Add scikit-learn to requirements (CCA + MI estimation)
Add scipy to requirements (entropy / pdist)
Optional: add torch + torchvision for CNN training
Optional: add transformers for CLIP fine-tuning
